subject: An emaciated, skeletal four-legged predator with a rabbit-like skull for a head — long upright ears, exposed bone, jagged teeth — prowling through a tile-floored corridor amid sprung mousetraps and ghostly white spectral claws rising from the ground. The creature embodies the sensation of being stalked by something patient and inescapable.
description: A gaunt, hairless predator-thing crouches in mid-stride across cracked beige floor tiles. Its head is a rabbit skull — elongated upright ears, hollow eye sockets, an exposed teeth-bared maw with the bone laid bare where flesh should be. Its body is stretched and emaciated, ribs and spine prominent under taut reddish skin, with patches of pale wraith-stuff trailing from its shoulders and back like smoke or peeled hide. The corridor curves around it in deep blood-red and rust tones, almost tunneling toward the viewer. Rising from the floor on both sides are ghostly white finger-claws and teeth — disembodied spectral hands reaching up from the tile as if the ground itself is hungry. Sprung mousetraps lie scattered on the floor at the creature's feet, their bait long taken. The lighting is hot and oppressive, lit as if from inside a furnace.
foreground: skeletal rabbit-skulled predator creature mid-prowl, sprung mousetraps on tiled floor, spectral white claws rising from ground
background: curving tunnel-like corridor of red-tinted walls, more spectral white claw-shapes lining the edges, deep red atmospheric haze
emotion: predatory menace, slow stalking patience, the snarl of something that already knows it's caught you
